summary: Erik's fishing-line idea: prove the walk can't drift back near itself (bounded returns) via EXCLUSION — triple-free self-exclusion + twist angular decorrelation + proven deep-tail separation — instead of counting

routes.route: ROUTE 1 — TRIPLE-FREE SELF-EXCLUSION: test whether two close, near-parallel passes through a Cheb-r ball (r<=10) are forbidden by triple-freeness, thereby bounding the pass count A0.
routes.proof: The mechanism is REFUTED by direct measurement at L6/L7 over all walk-centered balls (design/lemma/route1/selfexcl_probe.py). For every pair of maximal walk-arcs coexisting in a Cheb-r ball I measured the angle between their direction vectors, their Chebyshev separation, and the minimum |cross((a2-a1),(b-a1))| over triples with a1,a2 on one arc and b on the other (=0 would be a real collinear triple). Findings, level-stable L6->L7: (1) min coexisting-pair angle = 0.00 deg — passes routinely coexist EXACTLY parallel; even restricting to substantial arcs (both len>=3) the min angle is only ~2.0 deg with 7-15 such pairs under 10 deg. (2) The min triple-cross bottoms out at 1 (the smallest nonzero integer) at every rho — triple-freeness (cross!=0) is satisfied with the thinnest possible margin and imposes NO angular gap. (3) The supposed forbidden region (close AND near-parallel: theta<15, s<=rho/2) is heavily POPULATED (e.g. 82 pairs at L7 rho=9), not empty. Geometric reason: two lattice arcs can be exactly parallel and Chebyshev-close yet triple-free because they are laterally OFFSET — no point of one is collinear with any two of the other (cross=1, never 0). Near-parallel does NOT force a collinear triple; only exact overlap does, and offset parallel passes never overlap. Hence triple-freeness cannot bound A0.
routes.exclusion_force: Triple-freeness is the WRONG exclusion force for local pass-stacking. It forbids only cross=0 (exact collinear triple), which offset near-parallel passes never realize, so it permits unboundedly-stacked parallel passes in principle. What actually holds A0<=5 flat is METRIC: M-renormalization contraction (radius x4/9 per level), deep-tail separation (min|M^j v| = 3/8/24), the irrational twist arccos(-1/6), and the >=3-separated anchor set M.Z^3 — i.e. the single-level refill/anti-stacking lemma, not the walk's collinearity rule. kappa_3 is also locally vacuous (local kappa_3<=4 at r=10 vs the global <=12 needing chord distance ~515), so no Diophantine direction-packing tightens it either.
routes.A0_bound: From triple-freeness ALONE: only A0 <= geomcap(2r) = #primitive lattice directions of sup-norm<=2r = O(r^3) (144 / 1008 / 14256 at r=2/4/10) — loose by ~84x versus the measured, level-uniform A(r)<=5 on r in [1,10] (max_A: L5<=4, L6<=5, L7<=5, L8<=5; flat, does not grow with level). The tight A0<=5 is not derivable from triple-freeness; it is metric.
routes.computational_evidence: L6/L7 exact over ALL walk-centered Cheb-r balls, r=1..10. min coexisting-pair angle = 0.00 deg (both-len>=2), ~2.0 deg (both-len>=3); min_triple_cross = 1 at every rho (triple-free intact but razor-thin); close+near-parallel coexisting pairs abundant (L7 rho9: 82; L6 rho9: 33). Measured maxA (pass count): rho=[1..10] L6=[2,3,3,3,4,4,4,5,5,5], L7=[2,3,3,3,4,4,4,4,5,4] — flat <=5, level-stable, consistent with prior L8_arcs.json (A0<=5). Prior RESULTS.json independently reached STUCK (O(r^3), kappa_3 vacuous); this pass-level self-exclusion test confirms it and pinpoints WHY: parallel-offset passes are triple-free.
routes.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route1/selfexcl_probe.py (run: PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193 pypy3 design/lemma/route1/selfexcl_probe.py 6 7). Outputs: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route1/selfexcl-results.json (per-rho pairwise angle/sep/cross tables + densest-ball dumps) and /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route1/SELFEXCL-VERDICT.json.
routes.gap: The Route-1 premise is false: near-parallel + close passes do NOT force a collinear triple (they force only cross>=1, trivially satisfied by lateral offset on the integer lattice), so triple-freeness cannot exclude pass-stacking and yields only the useless O(r^3) direction-packing bound. The real, provable bound on returns does NOT come from triple-freeness at all; it reduces (per RETURNS-CONCLUSION.json, telescoping A(r) through exactly 4 M-levels since (4/9)^4*10<1/2) to the SAME single-level metric refill / anti-stacking lemma every other route stalls on: fresh per-level stitch arcs decorating the >=3-separated anchor walk have <= E*r points in any r-ball, E level-uniform (measured E<=3). Closing the whole proof requires promoting THAT metric lemma, which is outside triple-free self-exclusion's reach.

routes.route: ROUTE 2 — Twist angular decorrelation (exclusion argument, not counting)
routes.proof: REFUTED, not proven. The proposed exclusion force is: passes through a Cheb-r ball (r≤10) come from ≤3 adjacent birth levels (deep-tail, already proven), and adjacent-level passes cannot be near-parallel because M rotates each level's local directions by arccos(-1/6)=99.594° relative to the previous — so near-parallel crowding is impossible. I built birth-tagged L6/L7 chains (route4 decomposition Walk_L = ⊔_j M^j·interiors(L-j)), found dense balls, decomposed the path into maximal contiguous arcs (passes), tagged each pass by dominant birth level and direction (PCA + endpoint), and measured inter-/intra-level pass angles. The angular-gap premise is FALSE, for three independent reasons: (1) Same-level dominance — the return count is overwhelmingly WITHIN one birth level (L7 r=10: 171 same-level pass-pairs vs 20 adjacent-level pairs). M rotates only BETWEEN levels, so it cannot touch the dominant term at all, and same-level passes DO go near-parallel (min 3.92°; a verified pair of length-4 and length-12 fresh-level arcs at only 10.6° through one Cheb-10 ball). (2) No adjacent-level gap either — measured adjacent-level pass-angle minimum is 0.0° (L6 r=10) / 9.13° (L7 r=10), NOT bounded away from 0. (3) Structural cause — each birth level already carries a near-ISOTROPIC spread of arc directions (the 124-step menu spans all directions), and a rotation maps an isotropic ensemble into itself: for EVERY menu direction d, min_j angle(M·d, d_j) over the menu is ≤9.462° (often exactly 0). Rotating level-j's direction set by the twist reproduces essentially the same set — angular decorrelation between two rotation-invariant ensembles is vacuous. The "unspooling fishing line cannot drift back near-parallel" intuition simply does not hold for this walk.
routes.exclusion_force: The twist provides NO usable exclusion force. The x-axis (e1, eigenvalue 3) is a pure dilation — not rotated at all — so any x-aligned component of a direction is twist-invariant across all levels (measured pass x-alignment ~0.5 typical). Only the yz-plane rotates by 99.594°, but the arc-direction ensemble is isotropic enough that this rotation self-overlaps to within ≤9.5°. The ONLY genuine exclusion in play is deep-tail packing (min|M^3 v|∞ = 24 > 2r), which restricts passes to ≤3 adjacent birth levels — but that is a pre-existing metric/packing fact, not angular, and it does not bound the dominant same-birth-level return count. Triple-free forbids exact 3-collinearity but explicitly permits near-parallel returns, which is exactly what the data shows.
routes.A0_bound: Route 2 delivers NO new level-uniform bound. Measured A0 ≤ 5 (return count) is confirmed and level-stable across L6/L7 (max passes: L6 r=10 → 5, L7 r=10 → 4), matching route1/route4 — but this is NOT produced by angular separation, so Route 2 cannot certify it. The only lemma-free rigorous bound remains the cubic packing count O((2r/3+1)^3) on the ≥3-separated anchor set (useless). A linear level-uniform A0 still requires the single-level anti-stacking lemma, which Route 2 does not supply.
routes.computational_evidence: L6/L7 birth-tagged pass measurement (design/exclusion/route2-angular-results.json): adjacent-level pass-angle min = 0.0° (L6 r10) / 9.13° (L7 r10) — no gap. Same-level pass-pairs vastly outnumber adjacent (171 vs 20 at L7 r10); same-level near-parallel (<15°) events: 7/171 at L7 r10, min 3.92°; a verified substantial pair (len 4 & 12, birth 0) at 10.6°, dirs (-6,4,-1) & (20,-16,8). Pass x-alignment mean ~0.5 (large component on the non-rotating x-axis). Menu-isotropy test: for all 124 menu directions, min_j angle(M·d_i, d_j) ≤ 9.462° (many =0), and angle(M·e1,e1)=0 — the twist rotation reproduces the direction set, creating no angular separation. Max passes ≤5 (consistent with A0≤5). Far-level (Δbirth≥2) pass-pairs essentially absent (0 at r=5, 2 at L7 r10), reconfirming deep-tail ≤3 adjacent levels.
routes.script: design/exclusion/route2_angular.py (main measurement; PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193 pypy3 design/exclusion/route2_angular.py 6 7 → design/exclusion/route2-angular-results.json). Conclusion: design/exclusion/route2-CONCLUSION.json. Inline menu-isotropy test reproduced in the run log. Builds on gate_run.word_interiors and the route4 birth decomposition (design/lemma/route4_birth_telescope.py).
routes.gap: The twist cannot exclude self-approach: (a) it rotates only BETWEEN adjacent scales, but the return count is dominated by SAME-birth-level passes, which it leaves untouched; (b) even between adjacent levels there is no angular gap, because each level's arc-direction ensemble is near-isotropic and a rotation maps an isotropic set into itself (menu self-overlap ≤9.46°); (c) the x-axis is a non-rotating eigendirection, an outright loophole. The irreducible residue is unchanged from routes 1/3/4: the SINGLE-LEVEL REFILL / ANTI-STACKING LINEARITY lemma b_m(r) = sup_q #{level-m stitch interiors in Cheb-r} ≤ E·r + F (uniform in m). That lemma is about how the fresh connector-arcs of ONE level fail to stack in a small ball — a curve-like O(r) vs cubic O(r^3) incidence fact tied to triple-free — and Route 2's angular force gives no purchase on it (attacking it via the twist is vacuous under direction isotropy). Bounding the same-level return count / anti-stacking slope remains the open crux; angular decorrelation is not a route to it.

routes.route: ROUTE 3 — monotone escape / transience via a SPOOL COORDINATE. The right unspooling coordinate is the M-adapted log-radius u(p)=½·log₃(Q(p)) where Q(x,y,z)=x²+6y²−2yz+6z² is the invariant quadratic form of M=((3,0,0),(0,0,−3),(0,3,−1)). Key exact identity: Q(Mp)=9·Q(p) ⇒ u(Mp)=u(p)+1, verified exact on 10⁵ random integer vectors. Each M application "pays out" exactly one spool turn. A companion angle θ(p) (polar angle of (y,z) in the Q_yz-Cholesky-orthonormal frame) advances by the irrational rotation α=arccos(−1/6) per M. Together ζ=(u,θ): M is translation by (1,α) in log-spiral coordinates.
routes.proof: Transience derivation (what the spool delivers, non-circularly): Because u increases by EXACTLY +1 per M and (measured) has bounded backslide — min_i[u(p_{i+n})−u(p_i)] ≥ −b with b≈0.5 turn, level-stable/shrinking (L5 −0.53, L6 −0.44, L7 −0.18) vs the plain x-coordinate's useless −2n floor — the walk is weakly transient in u: it never reels the spool back more than ~half a turn. Combined with the proven deep-tail separation min|M^j v|∞=3/8/24, this confines the BIRTH LEVELS meeting any Cheb-≤10 ball to a band of ≤4 (measured, level-stable across L5/L6/L7). That excludes cross-scale STACKING — the only mechanism that could make A0 grow with k. So the k-uniform "far-return" arc count ≤ birthspread ≤ 4, proven from the u-identity + deep-tail bound alone, never counting the crowd. Residual: dissection of every dense ball shows the ≤5 arcs are NOT separated by u (min inter-arc u-gap = 0.0) nor by θ (spread < 0.01 rad) — same-shell returns are invisible to the spiral coordinate. Instead ALL crowding for r≤10 lives in ONE bounded path-window (max path-index span ≤70, level-uniform: L5 39 / L6 70 / L7 46 at r=10) with essentially no genuine far-returns (arcs separated by a gap > 2× ballistic sojourn: ≤1). So A0 = (far arcs ≤ birthspread ≤4, PROVEN by spool) combined with (local same-shell meander, bounded by the local-window span). The residual local-window bound is level-uniform in data but transience does NOT prove it.
routes.exclusion_force: Two distinct forces, cleanly separated by the experiment. (1) CROSS-SCALE (proven, non-circular): the spool identity u(Mp)=u(p)+1 with bounded backslide b≈0.5, plus deep-tail min|M^j v|∞=3/8/24, forces ≤4 birth levels per Cheb-≤10 ball — this is the transience/no-tangle that excludes far self-approach. (2) SAME-SHELL LOCAL (NOT transience): the spool AND the angle are both flat within a shell (u-gap 0, θ-spread <0.01), so monotone escape cannot exclude same-shell returns; those are bounded only by the already-proven diameter-ballistic sojourn (walk exits ballistically, giving the level-uniform ≤70 local-window span) together with the fixed finite connector-word alphabet. Triple-free is not the operative force for the dense balls; ballisticity + finite-alphabet self-similarity is.
routes.A0_bound: Measured A(q,r) ≤ 5 for all r∈[1,10], level-stable L5/L6/L7 (r=10: A0 = 4/5/4). PROVEN component (via spool): far/cross-scale arcs ≤ birthlevel-spread ≤ 4, level-uniform. RESIDUAL component: local same-shell meander arcs, bounded by the local path-window span ≤ ~70 (level-uniform in data, not yet proven). So A0 ≤ 5 is established computationally and its cross-scale half is proven k-uniformly; the same-shell half is reduced to one finite base-scale lemma.
routes.computational_evidence: All at L5/L6/L7 (chain lengths 8214/27697/92732), exact integer arithmetic. (1) Q(Mp)=9Q(p) exact on 10⁵ random vectors ⇒ u(Mp)=u(p)+1. (2) Anchors_k = M·chain_{k−1} verified exactly (8214/8214 M-images matched), giving the clean recursive birth-level labeling via M⁻¹ pullback. (3) u amortized backslide floor: L5 −0.53, L6 −0.44, L7 −0.18 (bounded, level-stable ⇒ weak transience; contrast x-coord −2n). (4) Per-ball arc decomposition r=1..10: A0 ≤5, birthlevel-spread ≤4, both level-stable. (5) min inter-arc u-separation = 0.0 and θ-spread <0.01 for all dense balls ⇒ spiral coordinate does NOT separate same-shell arcs. (6) max local path-span (r=10): 39/70/46, max far-arcs (gap>6r): 1/1/1 ⇒ crowding is a single bounded local window, no far-return stacking in range.
routes.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route3_spool.py (spool coordinate u+θ, M-invariance check, birth-level labeling via M⁻¹ pullback, per-ball arc/birthspread/A_base decomposition, u amortized-drift); /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route3_dissect.py (densest-ball dissection: per-arc birth level, u-range, θ-range, path-index range — reveals in-shell flatness); /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route3_span.py (per-ball path-index span, max inter-arc gap, far-arc count). Outputs: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route3-spool-results.json and /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route3-spool-CONCLUSION.json.
routes.gap: The residual is a SAME-SHELL / LOCAL-WINDOW NO-RETURN lemma: the path-index span of walk points inside any Cheb-≤10 ball is bounded by a level-independent constant W0 (≈70); equivalently, within a single shell the walk does not re-approach itself (Cheb≤10) except inside a bounded path-window, whence A(q,r) ≤ W0/2 + birthspread. The spool coordinate genuinely discharges the CROSS-SCALE half (birthspread ≤4, k-uniform, non-circular) and collapses the k-induction to this fixed-scale statement — but u and θ are provably FLAT within a shell, so monotone escape cannot prove the residual. It reduces to a finite check on the self-similar connector-word library (fixed length-≤5 alphabet) rather than the unbounded k-induction, so it is non-circular in kind, but it is still the anti-stacking residue localized to one shell and remains unproven. Next: prove the ≤70 local-window span from the diameter-ballistic sojourn S(n)≥0.64n plus finiteness of the connector alphabet (a bounded-configuration search), or find an in-shell secondary potential (θ is flat; a phase-unwound or triple-free-based coordinate is needed).

verdict.synthesis:
STRONGEST EXCLUSION PROOF (assembled from the surviving pieces of all three routes + the birth-telescoping/BOUND-1 machinery they lean on):

Target: A(q,r) = #distinct maximal walk-arcs meeting B(q,r) is bounded by a level-uniform A0 on r in [1,10], whence c_k(q,r) <= A0*L(r) <= A0*3.125r (sojourn L<=3.125r is PROVEN, diameter-ballistic).

The genuinely proven, non-circular backbone (this is the real engine, and it is metric/algebraic, NOT one of the three 'exclusion forces'):
1. EXACT birth partition: Walk_k = interiors(k) ⊔ M·interiors(k-1) ⊔ M²·interiors(k-2) ⊔ M³·Walk_{k-3}. Verified exact at L8 (4 parts, 0 overlap, union = chain). Gives c_k(q,r) = Σ_j t_j(q,r) EXACTLY.
2. DEEP-TAIL PACKING (proven, level-independent, no induction hypothesis): birth-shell-j ⊂ M^j Z³, so t_j <= P_j(r) = #{M^j Z³ ∩ Cheb-r box}. min|M^j v|_∞ = 3/8/24 for j=1/2/3. Since 24 > 2r for all r<=10, t_{>=3} <= 1 RIGOROUSLY. So the infinite k-recursion collapses to a FINITE 3-term sum: c_k(r) <= t_0(r) + t_1(4r/9) + t_2(4r/27) + 1.
3. This removes the DIVERGENT cross-scale feedback (the old 20/9>1 loop from charging refill through c_k) — the k-induction becomes a fixed-depth (4-level) telescope because (4/9)^4·10 < 1/2.

Route 3 (spool coordinate u=½log₃Q, Q(Mp)=9Q(p) so u(Mp)=u(p)+1, bounded backslide b≈0.5) reproduces this cross-scale discharge as 'weak transience' ⇒ birthspread <=4 per ball, non-circular. That is the strongest of the three exclusion routes and it is graded CLOSES-MODULO-CLEAN-LEMMA — but its proven content is co-extensive with the deep-tail packing already in BOUND-1.

THE RESIDUE (identical across ALL six routes, exclusion and counting alike): the SINGLE-LEVEL REFILL LINEARITY b_m(r) = sup_q #{level-m stitch interiors in Cheb-r} <= E·r + F, uniform in m. Measured E<=3, F<=1, flat across L6/L7/L8. This is where every route stops.
verdict.best_route: Route 3 (spool / transience) is the strongest of the three named exclusion routes — but the actual load-bearing closer is the birth-telescoping identity + BOUND-1 deep-shell packing (route4), which is exact counting/algebra, not an exclusion force. Route 3's proven half is a re-derivation of that same deep-tail packing.
verdict.best_A0: 5 (measured return count A(q,r) <= 5, level-stable L5-L8 on r in [1,10]; the rigorous telescoped crowding constant is c_k(r) <= E·S·r + O(1) = 3·1.677·r ≈ 5.03·r, with E the unproven single-level refill slope)
verdict.honest_assessment:
HONEST VERDICT: The exclusion reframing did NOT deliver the new purchase it promised, and it reduces to the same residue the counting routes hit — but that verdict comes with an important, genuinely-positive caveat that is NOT just renaming.

(a) Are the exclusion forces rigorous / do they bound returns, or reduce to parent-crowding? Two of the three exclusion forces are REFUTED outright, not merely stuck. TRIPLE-FREE (Route 1): near-parallel offset lattice arcs are triple-free (cross product = 1, never 0), so triple-freeness forbids only exact 3-collinearity and permits unboundedly-stacked parallel passes; it yields only the useless O(r³) direction-packing bound. Measured directly: min coexisting-pair angle = 0.00°, 82 close+near-parallel pairs in one L7 ball. TWIST (Route 2): the x-axis is a non-rotating eigenvector (eigenvalue 3), only the yz-plane rotates, and the arc-direction menu is near-isotropic so the rotation self-overlaps to <=9.46° — a rotation maps an isotropic ensemble into itself, giving ZERO angular gap. Worse, returns are SAME-birth-level-dominated (171 vs 20 pairs at L7 r10), and the twist only acts BETWEEN levels, so it cannot touch the dominant term. TRANSIENCE (Route 3): genuinely proves the CROSS-SCALE half (birthspread <=4, non-circular), but u and θ are provably FLAT within a shell (min inter-arc u-gap = 0.0, θ-spread < 0.01), so monotone escape is invisible to same-shell returns — the residual.

(b) Does deep-tail (<=3 adjacent levels) + triple-free/twist genuinely cap passes? NO. A configuration of near-parallel arcs slips through freely — explicitly exhibited: a length-4 and length-12 fresh-level (same birth) arc pair at only 10.6° through one Cheb-10 ball, triple-free intact. Within the <=3 admissible birth levels, same-level offset-parallel passes are abundant and neither triple-freeness nor the twist excludes them.

(c) The adversarial walk IS the real walk: on L6/L7/L8 the measurements show parallel-offset passes coexisting with triple-freeness razor-thin (cross=1). What actually holds A0<=5 flat is METRIC — M-renormalization contraction (radius ×4/9 per level) + deep-tail packing (min|M³v|=24>20) — NOT any of the three exclusion forces. Those metric facts alone give only a CUBIC/qualitative crowding law, not the tight linear A0.

(d) Bounded returns + proven sojourn: IF A0 were proven level-uniform, then c_k <= A0·3.125r is linear ⇒ availability floor ⇒ QED. The sojourn bound (L<=3.125r from S(n)>=0.64n) is PROVEN, and the whole rest of the reduction is proven and non-circular. So exactly ONE clean lemma stands between here and unconditional closure: the SINGLE-LEVEL REFILL LINEARITY b_m(r) <= E·r + F. It resists proof because the standalone packing bound is CUBIC (charging interiors to the >=3-separated anchor set packs the anchors as a cube (2(r+4)/3)³, giving a vacuous E≈316 on r<=10), while making it LINEAR needs the 'curve-not-cube' 1-D arc-incidence fact (only O(r), not O(r³), connector-arcs of one level meet a Cheb-r ball). Attacking b_m by anchor-charging re-introduces the parent-crowding loop (a_m ⇒ c_{m-1}); attacking by packing gives cubic. No exclusion force touches it.

DOES EXCLUSION BREAK THE CIRCULARITY? For the load-bearing residual: NO. All three exclusion forces provably fail on the same-shell single-level refill, which is the identical anti-stacking residue the counting routes died on. The one circularity that WAS genuinely broken — the divergent cross-scale 20/9 feedback — was broken by the EXACT birth partition + deep-tail lattice packing, which is counting/algebra, not the fishing-line exclusion intuition. So the exclusion strategy relocated and cleanly ISOLATED the residue (from an unbounded k-induction down to a fixed-scale, single-level, finite-connector-alphabet statement — real and valuable progress) but did not BREAK it. The residue is now clean IN KIND (a bounded finite check in principle) yet remains unproven, and the natural attacks recycle the same difficulty.
verdict.next: Attack the single-level refill linearity b_m(r) <= E·r + F directly as a FIXED-SCALE finite problem (it is no longer an unbounded induction — the birth-telescoping guarantees that). Concretely: (1) Formalize the 'curve-not-cube' 1-D arc-incidence claim — one level's stitch interiors form connector-arcs (fixed alphabet, words length <=5, steps Cheb<=2) decorating the >=3-separated anchor walk M·Walk_{m-1}; prove that a Cheb-r ball meets O(r) such arcs, not O(r³) anchors. The lever is that the anchor walk is itself a bounded-step PATH (a curve), so the anchors near the ball are not a full cube but a curve-crossing count — this needs the parent's linearity ONE level down, so set it up as: single-level refill slope E is bounded by the parent crowding SLOPE (linear coefficient), not the parent cube, closing the induction on the SLOPE rather than the count. (2) Alternatively, run an exhaustive bounded-configuration search over the finite connector-word library to certify b_m(r) <= 3r+1 as a base-case lattice computation, then lift by the exact birth telescope — check whether the finite alphabet genuinely bounds the number of same-shell arcs a ball can meet. (3) Abandon triple-free/twist as exclusion forces (both refuted); the only live 'exclusion' is transience, and it is flat in-shell — so the in-shell bound must come from a phase-unwound / arc-incidence potential, not monotone escape. Report which of (1)/(2) certifies E level-uniformly; that single lemma closes the unconditional proof.
